records.ety: [AS. cuhle, cugle, cugele; cf. dial. G. kogel, gugel, OF. coule, goule; all fr. LL. cuculla, cucullus, fr. L. cucullus cap, hood; perh. akin to celare to conceal, cella cell. Cf. Cucullate.]

defs: A monk's hood; -- usually attached to the gown. The name was also applied to the hood and garment together.
quotes: What differ more, you cry, than crown and cowl?

defs: A cowl-shaped cap, commonly turning with the wind, used to improve the draft of a chimney, ventilating shaft, etc.
A wire cap for the smokestack of a locomotive.
a removable metal covering for an aircraft engine, providing streamlining to minimize wind resistance; -- also called cowling.
a covering for a chimney or other ventilating shaft functioning to increase the draft.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. cuvele, cuvel, dim. of F. cuve tub, vat, fr. L. cupa. See Cup.]

defs: A vessel carried on a pole between two persons, for conveyance of water.
